The classification of Forasaccus purgans places it within the kingdom Plantae, and more specifically, within the clade of angiosperms, or flowering plants. It belongs to the order Caryophyllales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Forasaccus purgans is further classified as a member of the family Montiaceae, a family of plants commonly known as the "miner's lettuce" or "blite" family. This classification indicates the plant's evolutionary relationships and places it within a broader group of flowering plants.
